definition: Fed tightening strengthens USD, which lowers EM producer breakevens by 8-15% in local currency terms (BRL, CLP, ZAR, RUB), keeping marginal commodity supply online and capping USD commodity prices. This creates a validated structural dampener on commodity-channel monetary transmission.
evidence: 8-15% breakeven reduction in BRL, CLP, ZAR, RUB terms
Validated across Codelco, Vale, Anglo American Platinum, Russian crude
open_questions: Does the mechanism weaken as EM producers hedge FX exposure more actively?
How persistent is the dampening effect in a sustained strong-dollar regime?
